codeReview.summary: Pass. Reviewing the original failing commit `1b431eb` together with the fix commit `9575f72` shows the follow-up addresses the prior blocking issue directly: hosted-provider `supports_pty()` now reports backend capability from the shared provider spec instead of gating on `interactive_pty`, while the existing capability validator still rejects unsupported interactive PTY requests with the same clear error. The new regression test exercises both sides of the contract by proving a PTY-capable Daytona session created with default options still reports PTY support before and after resume, and a PTY-unsupported Runloop session still rejects an explicit PTY request.

summary: Reviewed the prior failed scrutiny report, both hosted-provider diffs, the follow-up handoff, the follow-up transcript skeleton, and the relevant mission shared-state guidance. The follow-up fix closes the PTY-capability gap without regressing unsupported-provider error handling, so this review passes with no new issues or shared-state gaps to record.
